finding: The peer execution runtime must acquire durable terminal ownership before appending a terminal event. A process-local map cannot prevent two independent runtimes from emitting conflicting terminals for the same trigger.
design_implication: Require a terminalJournal dependency, record the packet digest through its compare-and-set append contract, append the event only when the journal reports a fresh durable record, and fail closed on terminal conflict or invalid journal state.

verification.scope: isolated Node runtime using byte-identical candidate source before commit

uncertainty: The test backend implements the compare-and-set contract in memory; no production distributed backend has yet been exercised.
falsification_route: Run two independent OS processes against one production compare-and-set backend using the same trigger ID and different terminal events. More than one persisted terminal or more than one downstream append falsifies the guarantee.
privacy: No private chat, health, credential, or personal data included.
